figures.caption: Fig. 1: MINav enables rapid, two-hour deployment for realworld end-to-end ImageNav. (a) Autonomous data collection. (b) Offline policy learning. (c) Real-world policy deployment.
Fig. 2: Real-world deployment of MINav in the three evaluation environments. The robot successfully reaches image-goals despite clutter, blur, reflections, and partial occlusion.
TABLE I: Real-world ImageNav performance across three environments.

Fig. 3: Overview of the MINav pipeline. Starting from autonomous exploration in the target environment, MINav collects diverse trajectories using the proposed pink uniform noise model, extracts visual representations with frozen DINOv3, constructs the goal space, and trains a navigation policy offline via hindsight goal relabeling and TD3+BC.

Fig. 6: Visualization of exploration trajectories generated by different noise strategies. The corresponding normalized state entropy η(s), action entropy η(a), and state-action joint entropy η(s, a) are reported at the bottom of each subfigure, highlighting the superior coverage of the pink uniform strategy.
